description: Business that represents a customer or potential customer. The company that is billed in business transactions.
Person with whom a business unit has a relationship, such as customer, supplier, and colleague.
The bank that the branch is associated with.
The branch of the main bank, when borrower applies for a loan at a branch.
An association of several customers allowing e.g. the representation of households
An association between a customer and a Group.
Financial holdings owned by the customer.
A deposit account holding that allows the holder to make deposits and withdrawals through financial holding instruments. An account can be interest bearing
A preset borrowing limit associated with a credit or charge card.
A holding representing a portfolio of securities or other investible assets.
Loans held by the customer with the financial institution.
Savings accounts held by the customer at the financial institution.
A payment card issued by a bank to an account holder enabling the account holder to make payments or purchases, paying back the balance to the issuing bank, plus interest.
An automated payment system whereby an account holder authorizes a creditor to debit the customer's bank account at regular intervals.
An extension of credit associated with a checking account allowing the account holder to continue withdrawing funds when an account reaches zero.
An instruction order from an account holder for a set amount of money to be removed from an account at regular intervals and paid to or transferred to another account.
Accounts, loans, investments, credit lines and savings accounts held by a customer.
Financial tools associated with financial holdings; a monetary contract between parties which has intrinsic monetary value or transfers value. Provides an efficient flow and transfer of capital.
Financial holdings associated with a Group, to be included in the group's total assets and liabilities.
Significant milestones in a customer's personal life which can impact their financial situation, such as attending college, marriage, having a child, retirement, etc.

Denotes an association between one contact to another (not as part of the larger group) where there is not necessarily a direct association of finances. For example, a spouse, lawyer, child or grandparent.
